videos.title: The American Birkebeiner cross-country ski race
videos.description:
Since it was first held 50 years ago, the American Birkebeiner, a trek of more than 30 miles through the Northwoods of Wisconsin, modeled after a fabled bit of Norwegian history, has become the largest cross-country ski race in the U.S. Correspondent Conor Knighton reports on how enthusiasm for the festival of events surrounding the race (dubbed "Birke fever") has snowballed.
#Birkebeiner #crosscountryski 

"CBS Sunday Morning" features stories on the arts, music, nature, entertainment, sports, history, science and Americana, and highlights unique human accomplishments and achievements. Check local listings for CBS Sunday Morning broadcast times.

videos.transcript:
it's part ski Marathon part Norwegian
History part Street Festival Connor
Knighton takes us to the starting line
of a race like none other
s
[Music]
each winter thousands of cross-country
skiers from all across the world travel
to the North Woods of Wisconsin to
travel more than 30 miles between the
towns of cable and Hayward
the American birkabiner is the largest
cross-country ski race in the country
and the enthusiasm here is contagious we
all got the Berkey fever and it's just
the excitement that builds about not
only the race but the whole event of
Berkey week the official burkabiner race
Berkey for short could fly by in a
couple of hours for the most elite
skiers the leading up to the main event
there are days of activities there are
shorter races for children
foreign
dog lovers ski with their pets teams of
six compete in giant ski races it's kind
of like Comic-Con for athletes is how
I'm looking at it Jackie and Dan daroma
are Avid skiers Jackie has skied the
Berkey four times Dan 17 but for this
year's race they wanted to do things a
little differently these other Racers
are going to be in spandex Gore-Tex you
know what are you gonna be wearing full
on fur
Jackie Dan and Dan's Father Jim were
selected to be mascots of sorts skiing
the entire course on wooden skis dressed
as Norwegian folk Heroes all while
carrying a baby doll it's a tradition
with roots and a far older history
as portrayed in the film The Last King
in the early 13th century Norway was in
the middle of a civil war after King
hokan III died members of the birkaviner
faction skied his son the heir to the
throne through the woods to safety
each year in Wisconsin three skiers
reenact the rescue I think the the fact
that there is this real story about an
event that now you're recreating it I
think that's pretty darn cool and pretty
special and I think it resonates with a
lot of people Ben pop is the executive
director of the American burkabiner
which is modeled after a Norwegian race
there every competitor skis with a baby
weight backpack to honor the Warriors
journey of course you may be asking
yourself why is this crazy Norwegian
history have anything to do with Hayward
Wisconsin in the middle of Northwestern
Wisconsin nowhere it all came down to
one man Tony wise
Tony wise was born in Hayward and was a
born marketer after serving in World War
II he decided to bring the competitive
skiing he observed in Europe back home
to Wisconsin a place where a
Scandinavian ancestry is common as a way
to encourage tourism his first Berkey
race held 50 years ago had just 35
participants most of whom had never
cross-country skied before I swore up
and down after that first I would never
do anything so foolish again but
today at age 75
Ernie St Germain is the only competitor
who's participated in every single race
but there are more than 2 000 athletes
who have appeared in 20 or more contests
which can turn into grueling all-day
endeavors
the Berkey attracts skiers of varying
ages and ability levels
you're really gonna discover who you are
it's one of the toughest Marathon
courses in the world so let yourself
kind of Glide a little there you go good
that challenge is what attracts Elite
skiers like Olympian Caitlyn Gregg who's
won the women's race a record setting
five times the Berkey course is unlike
any other ski course that we get to race
not only here in the United States but
also in around the world and I think
that it's terrain is so unique and so
challenging that people just myself
included love that aspect of it
the race itself has faced challenges
posed by warmer Winters in recent
decades it's been canceled twice due to
lack of snow it's really important that
we look at climate change and weather
because if we don't have it well it's a
big loss the event provides a major
boost for the local economy this town of
fewer than 3 000 residents hosts close
to 40 000 Spectators on race day
hayward's Main Street overflows with
cheering fans caught up in Berkey fever
for which the only prescription is more
cowbell
the church bells signal the arrival of
the first finisher David Norris won this
year's men's race with a commanding lead
Caitlyn Gregg came in third in the
women's division
the biggest cheers of the day were
reserved for the dromas nearly 7
after leaving the starting line they
skied onto Main Street and swapped out
the baby doll for an actual baby
their daughter Clara played the part of
Prince Hogan for the final few blocks
the burkabiners saved the day
until
do it all again next year
I'll be ready to go

videos.title: Champion Hill nordic ski trails
videos.description: Friday morning at Champion Hill nordic ski trails with the Betsie Bike Club.
videos.transcript:
hi this is ken lake i'm at champion hill
nordic ski trails on a chris friday
morning with the betsy bike club
they're going skiing and i'm going
inside to talk to amy
my family enjoys skiing and we realize
that we have great
terrain for cross-country skiing we're
located in the highlands of benzie
county
and there's elevation differences of up
to 150 feet throughout the property
and we have over 370 acres here so we
were able to put in
more than 23 kilometers of trails
most of which do not cross the golf
course and are very sheltered
and they wind through open rolling and
wooded terrain
and despite the elevation differences we
have trails groomed
for very beginner skiers all the way up
to advanced skiers
and we do groom for both skate and
classic skiing
our most popular trail is the scenic
trail it's about seven kilometers in
length
and it's well known because it has
scenic overlooks
that have views of lake michigan the
platte lakes
the empire bluffs and on sonata if
you're lucky on a clear day you can see
south manitou island
we have a large heated nordic center a
lot of skiers like to come in when
they're done skiing
and enjoy one of our complimentary hot
drinks grandma's hot spiced cider is
very popular
and uh we have group rates we just
started offering twilight rates so start
at 4 pm
um i guess do you rent equipment here
yes we have um equipment rental and we
have a ski waxing table
and we also have a small amount of wax
and stuff that we sell here
do you give lessons no we don't give
lessons yet that might be something that
we'll look into for the future
or maybe bring somebody here at some
point that could be this is a fantastic
lodge actually the view from here is
really beautiful
yeah as we're standing here overlooking
all the trails and it's a glorious day
here in
mid-january yeah
now you ski you say as well yes i do do
you have a favorite trail
my favorite trail is probably the
switchback trail it winds through the
woods it has some
fun ups and downs um so that would be my
favorite trail
this is a spectacular property now the
property's been in the family for a long
time um
are you folks from here yeah we're from
here pinecroft was
family orchards and my grandfather
was born on the property of pinecraft
and we just recently acquired property
that's adjacent to champion hill which
is where my grandmother was born
so they both go way back oh wow in the
family
well excellent i think this is just a
fantastic site i can't wait to get out
there

videos.title: Ski Skating an Mt Bachelor Nordic
Our Champions - Sara Renner
videos.description:
ATB Financial sponsors Sara Renner for the 2010 winter Olympics in Vancouver. Shot in Canmore, AB.

Filmed with a Panasonic HVX-200A.

The video can be seen on it's original website at www.wearealberta.ca
videos.transcript:
[Music]
[Applause]
Well, I basically grew up on skis. Um,
my parents run a backcountry lodge
called Mount Cineboy and it was 28 km
from the nearest road. So, to get
anywhere to play or get out to go to
school, we would ski. For Sarah Rener,
skiing changed from a way to get around
to a full-blown passion when the 1988
Winter Olympics came to her hometown of
Canmore. As a 10-year-old kid, it just
blew my socks off. I thought it was the
coolest thing I'd ever seen in my life.
And uh at the same time, it made me a
little sad because there was no
Canadians on the podium. and I I could
really close my eyes and and dream that
I could be one day a Canadian on the
podium in cross country skiing. And
that's exactly what she did. After years
of persevering on the World Cup cross
country ski circuit, Sarah won a silver
medal at the 2006 Olympics in Torino.
Following the games, Sarah put her
skiing career on hold to start a family.
Now a mom, Sarah wasn't sure if she
could handle the physical and mental
strain of training for Vancouver 2010.
But she decided to give it a go. As I
progressed, it was incredible how much I
my love for my sport came back. That was
that was what I really that's what
really drove me is that this is
something that I love to do and I
haven't accomplished my ultimate goal,
which is which is to win. When she
arrived back on the World Cup circuit in
2007, she did it with her 2-year-old
daughter, Arya. Sometimes I thought,
"Okay, this is the most ridiculous thing
anyone could ever choose to do. What am
I doing here? Changing diapers and
worrying about having enough food while
I'm warming up for a ski race." So, it
definitely had its moments, but it
seemed to work for Sarah. She managed
four top 10 finishes last season and
feels she's ahead of where she was when
training for Torino four years ago.
Maybe the combination of being a mom is
giving me this really great perspective
where I can do really good training and
and I come home and I have this really
good distraction from sport and uh
that's important especially for an
Olympics at home to have this
perspective. There's more to life than
just skiing. It's it's what I do but uh
I have other things that I I feel
passionate about. To help her focus on
her two passions, ATB Financial has
stepped up with a new corporate
sponsorship. But there was a catch. Two.
One.
She had to race President and CEO Dave
Mow down Calgary Steven Avenue on
rollerblades. For to be an athlete and
to be recognized and to be supported,
especially by an Alberta company, it's
it's really important. It it makes a
difference and um especially leading up
to the games in Vancouver to have uh not
only the financial support but the
support of the employees and uh the
clients of ATB I think it's it's quite a
powerful powerful thing.
